zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith於1206年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯時，他的父親Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith44歲，母親Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith21歲。他和Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1283年2月5日在蘇格蘭度福利爾藍霍爾姆享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith于1206年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯时，他的父亲Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith44岁，母亲Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith21岁。他和Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1283年2月5日在苏格兰度福利爾藍霍爾姆享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.
Scottish: variant of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith was born in 1206, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith, was 44 and his mother, Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, was 21. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. He died on 5 February 1283, in Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith est né en 1206, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. Il est décédé le 5 février 1283, à Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith 1206 in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith 44 und seine Mutter Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith 21. Er hatte mit Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Februar 1283 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith è nato il nel 1206 nel Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. Morì il 5 febbraio 1283, nel Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeithが1206年にスコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで生まれた時，父Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeithは44歳，母Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeithは21歳であった。彼はCountess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeithとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1283年2月5日にスコットランドダムフリーシュアイアラングhオルムで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith(이)가 1206년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1283년 2월 5일 스컷랜드 드프리실르 랭홀름에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith nasceu em 1206, em Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. Ele faleceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1283, em Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith родился в 1206 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, 21. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. Он умер 5 февраля 1283 г. в местности лангхолм, думфриессхир, Шотландия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Henry de Graham of Abercon - Baron of Dalkeith nació en 1206, en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, su papá, Sir Henry de Graham - Baron of Dalkeith, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Lady Christiana Fil Ada de Tarbolton - Lady Graham of Dalkeith, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con Countess Idonea de Avenel of Dalkeith. Murió el 5 de febrero de 1283, en Langholm, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, a la edad de 77 años.
